Ad Text:
Lead AI Engineer

Melbourne, VIC | Full-Time | Hybrid / Flexible Working

Build real-world AI at scale

We’re partnering with a fast-growing organisation in the digital identity and data space, delivering intelligent, data-driven products used in real-world environments.

They’re seeking a Lead AI Engineer to drive the design, development, and delivery of production-grade AI solutions - combining technical leadership with hands-on engineering.

This is a rare opportunity to work with modern LLM architectures and shape how AI is built, deployed, and scaled across a high-impact platform.


Key Responsibilities
 
Lead the design and delivery of end-to-end AI solutions
Build and deploy LLM-powered systems (RAG, agents, orchestration)
Develop scalable APIs, services, and data pipelines
Design retrieval and ranking systems for accurate outputs
Establish AI evaluation, testing, and quality frameworks
Drive LLMOps practices (monitoring, performance, optimisation)
Ensure solutions are secure, reliable, and cost-efficient
Mentor engineers and contribute to technical leadership

Skills & Experience
 
Proven experience building production-grade software systems
Strong hands-on experience with LLMs / GenAI technologies
Expertise in backend development (APIs, microservices, data systems)
Experience with RAG, prompt design, or agent-based workflows
Strong understanding of system performance, scalability, and reliability
Experience with AI evaluation and quality improvement
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Nice to Have
 
Experience with search / retrieval systems or vector databases
Knowledge of AI cost optimisation techniques
Exposure to secure or data-sensitive environments
Background in SaaS or data-rich platforms

What’s on Offer
 
Opportunity to lead and shape AI capability
Work with cutting-edge AI technologies in production
Collaborative and high-performing team environment
Flexible / hybrid working arrangements
Strong focus on innovation, impact, and growth
 

Apply Now

If you’re a hands-on AI leader looking to build impactful solutions at scale, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now and HIT the APPLY BUTTON or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Ad Text:
Product Design Manager, Artificial Intelligence (AI)

Permanent Full-Time 
Hybrid flexibility for work-life balance | Chatswood, St Leonards or Charlestown
Attractive salary, up to $175,917 + 12% Super + 17.5% annual leave loading
Up to 12 extra days off per year + 4 weeks annual leave
Increase your take home pay with salary packaging, meal allowance, fitness passport and more
Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation

Applications Close:  11:59pm, Wednesday 8th April 2026

About Us 
At eHealth NSW, we’re leading the digital transformation of Australia’s largest health system. As NSW Health’s digital centre of excellence, we design, build and support world-class ICT solutions that improve patient care across the state.

Guided by a Cloud First Strategy, we’re harnessing digital technology to better patient care and deliver scalable, quality, patient-centred, digital healthcare. With your skills and experience, you can help enrich the lives of millions while developing your career within Australia’s largest health organisation—alongside passionate tech professionals, health partners and industry leaders building solutions that truly make a difference.

About the Opportunity

AI is transforming our NSW Health system, and we are working to harness its use to address some of our most complex challenges so we can deliver safer, smarter and more connected care for the people of NSW.  

 

As the AI Product Manager, you will lead a team responsible for the early-stage creation, development, and validation of multiple AI enabled products and platforms. This role applies deep technical and product expertise to define customer problems, experiment with emerging AI capabilities, prototype and pilot solutions, and embed responsible AI and clinical safety requirements across discovery, delivery, and operation.  

You will coordinate cross‑functional delivery with Design/UX, Engineering, Data Science/MLOps, Architecture and Clinical Safety teams to deliver increments, manage dependencies and enable reuse of components and patterns as well as support the planning and management of releases.

As a subject matter expert, the role contributes to the vision, strategy, compliance and governance of all AI solutions.

For more information, please refer to the Role Description 

About You
We are looking for someone with:

Demonstrated experience managing a digital product end‑to‑end (vision, roadmap, backlog, delivery, benefits) in an agile environment.
Practical understanding of AI/ML in products: model evaluation and monitoring, MLOps, data governance and privacy‑by‑design, bias/robustness, prompt/LLM patterns 
Strong stakeholder engagement and change leadership skills with the ability to influence across clinical, operational and technical groups.
Recognised qualifications and/or equivalent experience in product management, AI/ML, data science, computer science, health informatics or related field, and a record of achievement in an ICT or health service organisation.
